solutions.solution: (a) Conclusions I. Some avenues being streets is a possibility. (✓) II. Some countries are flags. (✗)
(d) Conclusions I. No avenue is a country. (✗) II. Some streets are flags. (✗)
(d) Conclusions I. All lunch has gravy. (✓) II. All lunch has vegetable. (✗)
(b) Conclusions I. Some blue is pink. (✗) II. Some green is pink. (✓)
(d) Conclusions I. All boys are Punjabi II. Some boys are Punjabi (✗)
(e) Conclusions I. Some yellow are red. (✓) II. Some green are red. (✓) III. Some red are not green. (✗) IV. All green are red. (✗)
(d) Conclusions I. Some centuries are years. (✗) II. Some decades are years. (✓) III. No century is a year. (✓)

(b) Conclusions I. Some hockey players are football players. (✓) II. No basketball player is a cricketer. (✓) III. Some hockey players are basketball players. (✗) IV. Some football players are cricketers. (✓)
(c) Conclusions I. Some stones are pearls. (✗) Complementary pair (I-E) II. Some corals are diamonds. (✗) III. No stone is pearl. (✗) Complementary pair (I-E)
(e) Conclusions I. Some rocks are cement. (✗) II. Some rocks are sands. (✗) III. Some sands are bricks. (✗) IV. No brick is cement (✗)
(d) Conclusions I. Some cars are states. (✗) II. Some cups are cars. (✗) Complementary pair (I-E) III. Some apples are cups. (✓) IV. No car is cup. (✗) Complementary pair (I-E)
